Title: "Kea HA Strategies Comparison"
Description: ""Introduction Kea's High Availability hook is the most popular solution for high availability operation. The Kea HA hook works by pairing Kea servers (a multi-node solution is also available), in either an active-active or active-passive collaboration scheme. In this way, the Kea servers can monitor each other and assume responsibility for answering on behalf of the other server in case of failure. It is also possible for multiple Kea servers to leverage the lease 'backend' feature to share a sin ...""

Title: "Kea: Use unique databases"
Description: ""Summary When configuring Kea to use a database for storage of leases or host reservations, use a unique database for each Kea server. Within a single Kea high availability group (HA group), the database may be shared, subject to certain considerations. Guidance Use a unique database for every Kea server. A database can be made unique by creating a different database name on the same database server, or by using different database servers. Jun 23 , 12:15 EDT Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 23 , 10:49 EDT Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jun 23 , 10:37 EDT Investigating - Some ShareFile customers may be experiencing issues with AI File Renaming and AI File Validation for Document Request Lists. We are investigating this issue and we will provide updates here as soon as available.""

Title: "Kea files and directories"
Description: ""Introduction This article identifies the major files and directories used by Kea, and addresses security aspects. To maximize security, files and directories used by Kea should have the most restrictive ownership and permissions practical while still allowing desired function. Administrators should also read Kea Security for other essential security information. Mandatory Access Control regimes like SELinux and AppArmo ...""
